channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: The World Around Young Climate Prize 2026: Global Mentorship, Fellowship & New York Summit Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders Under 25
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the third cycle of The World Around’s Young Climate Prize 2026, a prestigious global mentorship, accelerator, and fellowship programme designed to support exceptional young climate innovators, activists, designers, and changemakers working on the front lines of the climate crisis. The programme identifies and accelerates self-initiated climate action projects led […]

The post The World Around Young Climate Prize 2026: Global Mentorship, Fellowship & New York Summit Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders Under 25 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the third cycle of The World Around’s Young Climate Prize 2026, a prestigious global mentorship, accelerator, and fellowship programme designed to support exceptional young climate innovators, activists, designers, and changemakers working on the front lines of the climate crisis.

The programme identifies and accelerates self-initiated climate action projects led by young people aged 25 and under from anywhere in the world. Through mentorship, masterclasses, international networking, strategic support, and global exposure, the Young Climate Prize empowers the next generation of climate leaders to strengthen and scale their impact.

The application deadline for the 2026 Young Climate Prize Cycle 03 is June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM EDT.

The programme is free to enter and participation is completely free for selected applicants.

About The World Around Young Climate Prize

The Young Climate Prize is a global mentorship and award programme created by The World Around, an international nonprofit organization focused on design, architecture, climate action, and social innovation. The initiative aims to identify, support, and amplify young people already creating meaningful climate solutions within their communities.

Every two years, 25 outstanding young changemakers under the age of 25 are selected through an international open call. These participants represent diverse regions, communities, and climate realities from around the globe.

The programme combines:

  • One-on-one mentorship
  • Climate leadership development
  • Design Academy masterclasses
  • International networking
  • Professional development
  • Global visibility
  • Fellowship opportunities
  • Public presentation opportunities

The programme culminates at The World Around Summit in New York City, where selected winners present their work to an international audience of designers, policymakers, educators, climate experts, and global leaders.

What Makes the Young Climate Prize Unique?

Unlike many climate competitions focused only on ideas or proposals, the Young Climate Prize specifically supports young people who are already leading live projects with measurable climate impact in their communities.

The programme is particularly unique because it:

  • Prioritizes real-world climate action
  • Focuses on mentorship and long-term support
  • Supports interdisciplinary climate solutions
  • Uses design as a tool for climate innovation
  • Builds a global youth climate network
  • Continues support through a three-year fellowship programme

The initiative recognizes that young people are not just future leaders—they are already leading climate action today.

Who Can Apply?

The Young Climate Prize is open globally to young people actively working on climate solutions.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  1. Be between 13 and 25 years old at the time of application
  2. Be the founder or co-founder of a live climate-related project
  3. Be actively leading the project at the time of application
  4. Demonstrate measurable or visible community impact
  5. Submit a climate-focused initiative addressing climate change mitigation or adaptation

Team Applications

Groups of up to three people may apply together. However:

  • Every team member must meet all eligibility requirements
  • Each participant must fall within the age range
  • All members must actively contribute to the project

Global Eligibility

Applicants may come from:

  • Any country
  • Any continent
  • Any community or region worldwide

The programme welcomes participants from:

  • Urban communities
  • Indigenous communities
  • Coastal regions
  • Rural communities
  • Climate-vulnerable areas
  • Marginalized populations

What Types of Projects Are Eligible?

The programme accepts a wide variety of climate-related projects and creative practices.

Eligible projects may include:

  • Climate advocacy campaigns
  • Environmental education initiatives
  • Sustainable architecture projects
  • Circular economy solutions
  • Waste reduction systems
  • Urban resilience initiatives
  • Indigenous climate knowledge preservation
  • Renewable energy innovations
  • Community adaptation projects
  • Climate storytelling
  • Conservation efforts
  • Food systems innovation
  • Biodiversity protection
  • Sustainable fashion projects
  • Climate communication platforms
  • Ecological design projects

The programme places special emphasis on design as a driver of climate solutions.

Programme Structure

1. One-on-One Mentorship

Each selected participant is paired with an internationally recognized mentor whose expertise, network, and experience align with the participant’s work.

Mentorship sessions help participants:

  • Strengthen project strategy
  • Improve communication
  • Expand project visibility
  • Access global networks
  • Scale impact
  • Refine storytelling
  • Build professional confidence

Young Climate Prize Design Academy

Selected participants also join the Young Climate Prize Design Academy, a weekly learning and development programme featuring:

  • Workshops
  • Masterclasses
  • Talks
  • Strategy sessions
  • Professional development training

The academy focuses on:

  • Climate leadership
  • Communication
  • Design thinking
  • Public engagement
  • Advocacy
  • Storytelling
  • Project scaling
  • Systems thinking

Global Experts & Mentors

The Design Academy masterclasses are led by members of The World Around’s global network of designers, educators, architects, curators, and climate thinkers.

Notable mentors and contributors include:

  • Paola Antonelli
  • Bruce Mau
  • Mariam Issoufou
  • Douglas Cardinal
  • Alice Rawsthorn
  • Al Borde
  • Carson Chan
  • Dominique Petit-Frère
  • Kotchakorn Voraakhom

These globally respected experts provide insights across:

  • Architecture
  • Design
  • Sustainability
  • Climate communication
  • Urbanism
  • Ecology
  • Landscape design
  • Community development

Final Awards & International Recognition

At the conclusion of the programme, an international jury selects:

  • Young Climate DesignerRemove featured image
  • Young Climate Visionary
  • Young Climate Voice

The selected winners receive the opportunity to:

  • Travel to New York City
  • Present their work at The World Around Summit 2027
  • Gain international exposure
  • Connect with global climate leaders
  • Showcase their projects to influential audiences

Three-Year Fellowship Opportunity

One of the strongest aspects of the programme is that all cohort participants automatically transition into the Young Climate Prize Fellowship after completing the programme cycle.

The fellowship provides:

  • Three years of ongoing support
  • Continued mentorship
  • Access to resources
  • Networking opportunities
  • Professional growth support
  • Project development opportunities
  • Global collaborations

This long-term structure distinguishes the programme from one-time competitions or awards.

What Participants Gain

Selected participants receive several benefits throughout the programme.

Benefits Include:

  • Personalized mentorship
  • Access to world-renowned climate and design leaders
  • Weekly Design Academy masterclasses
  • Global networking opportunities
  • Climate leadership development
  • Storytelling and communication training
  • Strategic guidance for project growth
  • Exposure to international audiences
  • Fellowship opportunities
  • Potential travel to New York City
  • Opportunity to present at The World Around Summit
  • Connection to a global climate innovation community

Submission Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  1. A short introductory video about their project
  2. Written or visual materials showing evidence of impact
  3. Supporting documentation demonstrating project work

Submissions may be completed in:

  • English
  • Spanish

Application Deadline

June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM EDT

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid technical issues and to ensure enough time to prepare strong submissions.

How to Apply

Applicants can apply through the official Young Climate Prize application platform.

Apply to Young Climate Prize Cycle 03 here. The World Around also welcomes nominations here.

Why Young Climate Leaders Should Apply

Climate change is one of the defining global challenges of our time, and young people are already creating transformative solutions within their communities.

The Young Climate Prize offers:

  • Recognition
  • Mentorship
  • International exposure
  • Long-term fellowship support
  • Leadership development
  • Professional growth
  • Community building

For young changemakers already taking action, this programme provides the tools, guidance, and network needed to expand their impact globally.

Learn more about the Young Climate Prize 2026 HERE.

Conclusion

The World Around Young Climate Prize 2026 is more than just a competition—it is a long-term global accelerator and fellowship programme designed to empower young people shaping the future of climate action.

By combining mentorship, education, storytelling, design, leadership, and global collaboration, the programme creates a powerful platform for youth-led climate innovation worldwide.

Young climate leaders, designers, activists, and community innovators under 25 who are already creating meaningful environmental impact should strongly consider applying before the June 30, 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Fellowship, Climate Action and Competition opportunities on OFY.

The post The World Around Young Climate Prize 2026: Global Mentorship, Fellowship & New York Summit Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders Under 25 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Darwin Initiative Round 32 Grants 2026 — Up to £5 Million Funding for Global Biodiversity, Climate & Poverty Reduction Projects
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Darwin Initiative Round 32 funding call is now officially open, offering grant opportunities of up to £5 million to support innovative projects that protect and restore biodiversity while also addressing poverty reduction and sustainable economic development. Funded by the UK Government through the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (DEFRA), the Darwin Initiative […]

The post Darwin Initiative Round 32 Grants 2026 — Up to £5 Million Funding for Global Biodiversity, Climate & Poverty Reduction Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Darwin Initiative Round 32 funding call is now officially open, offering grant opportunities of up to £5 million to support innovative projects that protect and restore biodiversity while also addressing poverty reduction and sustainable economic development.

Funded by the UK Government through the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (DEFRA), the Darwin Initiative is one of the world’s longest-running and most impactful biodiversity funding programmes. Since its launch in 1993, it has invested over £289 million across 1,370+ projects in more than 150 countries.

This funding round is designed to support high-impact conservation projects that not only protect ecosystems but also strengthen livelihoods, governance systems, and long-term sustainability in biodiversity-rich regions.

About the Darwin Initiative

The Darwin Initiative is part of the Biodiversity Challenge Funds, supporting countries and communities to develop practical, scalable solutions to biodiversity loss.

It focuses on:

  • Conservation and restoration of nature
  • Poverty reduction through sustainable resource use
  • Strengthening local and national environmental governance
  • Supporting climate resilience and ecosystem-based adaptation
  • Promoting sustainable economic growth through biodiversity protection

Rather than short-term interventions, the programme prioritizes systemic, long-term impact across landscapes and communities.

Funding Available (Round 32)

Applicants can access funding depending on the scheme:

Funding Levels:

  • Up to £5 million per project

Scheme Types:

  • Darwin Initiative Extra
  • Darwin Initiative Main
  • Darwin Initiative Capability & Capacity

Each scheme has different eligibility rules, timelines, and application stages.

Key Focus Areas for Round 32

This funding round introduces updated priorities to improve global impact and efficiency.

1. Biodiversity Hotspot Targeting

Applications must focus on 13 priority biodiversity hotspots across 35 countries, ensuring:

  • Higher impact concentration
  • Stronger ecosystem protection outcomes
  • Reduced fragmentation of funding efforts
  • Better coordination among projects

2. Systems-Level Change Approach

Projects are encouraged to move beyond direct interventions and instead strengthen:

  • Environmental governance systems
  • Institutional frameworks
  • Market systems linked to conservation
  • Long-term sustainability mechanisms

This shift ensures that outcomes remain impactful even after project completion.

3. Reduced Application Burden

To improve accessibility:

  • Stage 1 applications (Extra & Main) are now shorter concept notes
  • Only high-potential projects progress to Stage 2

4. Funding Structure Changes

  • Innovation scheme removed (now covered by GCBC – Global Centre on Biodiversity for Climate)
  • More funding redirected to remaining schemes
  • Stronger emphasis on quality and strategic alignment

5. Lead Applicant Limitation

To encourage fairness and local leadership:

  • Organisations may lead only one application per scheme
  • Partners can still join multiple applications

Programme Impact and Achievements

Since 1993, the Darwin Initiative has achieved:

  • £289 million invested globally
  • 1,370+ conservation projects funded
  • Millions of hectares of ecosystems protected
  • Over 100,000 people supported in climate resilience
  • Major biodiversity protection across forests, wetlands, and marine ecosystems

Example Impact:

In Indonesia, Darwin-funded projects have helped:

  • Manage 1.3 million hectares of forests and fisheries sustainably
  • Contribute to a 12.6 million-hectare UNESCO Biosphere Reserve
  • Improve regional biodiversity governance and planning

Webinar: Round 32 Funding Updates

A dedicated webinar will provide insights into the latest updates and application changes.

Webinar Details:

  • Date: 28 May 2026
  • Time: 6:00 pm (Vietnam Time) / 12:00–1:00 pm UK time (GMT+1)
  • Format: Online briefing session

What You Will Learn:

  • Key updates for Round 32
  • Changes in application structure and priorities
  • New geographic focus areas
  • Guidance on eligibility and funding schemes
  • Opportunity for Q&A

Application Deadlines (Round 32)

Darwin Initiative Main

  • Stage 1: 20 July 2026
  • Stage 2 (invite only): 30 November 2026

Darwin Initiative Extra

  • Stage 1: 1 July 2026
  • Stage 2 (invite only): 2 November 2026

Capability & Capacity

  • Single Stage Deadline: 31 August 2026

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Applicants are strongly advised to:

  • Read full guidance documents carefully
  • Select the correct scheme (Extra, Main, or Capability & Capacity)
  • Align proposals with biodiversity hotspot priorities
  • Focus on systems-level impact and sustainability
  • Ensure strong local partnerships and implementation capacity

 

Click here for more global grant opportunities.

The post Darwin Initiative Round 32 Grants 2026 — Up to £5 Million Funding for Global Biodiversity, Climate & Poverty Reduction Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: YLabs Youth Leadership Fellow 2026: Remote Fellowship Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Digital Health and Youth Advocacy
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
YLabs is now accepting applications for the Youth Leadership Fellow position under its innovative digital youth wellness initiative, AskingforaFriend.org (AFAF). This exciting remote fellowship opportunity is designed for passionate young leaders across the United States who are committed to supporting adolescent health, youth empowerment, mental wellness, and inclusive digital resources for marginalized communities. The fellowship […]

The post YLabs Youth Leadership Fellow 2026: Remote Fellowship Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Digital Health and Youth Advocacy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
YLabs is now accepting applications for the Youth Leadership Fellow position under its innovative digital youth wellness initiative, AskingforaFriend.org (AFAF). This exciting remote fellowship opportunity is designed for passionate young leaders across the United States who are committed to supporting adolescent health, youth empowerment, mental wellness, and inclusive digital resources for marginalized communities.

The fellowship offers young people the chance to directly shape a national youth-focused platform that addresses critical issues affecting adolescents, including mental health, sexual health, gender-based violence (GBV), identity affirmation, and access to supportive care resources.

Successful applicants will receive compensation of up to USD 5,000 per month while working remotely with a globally recognized youth innovation organization.

Applications close on 05 June 2026.

About YLabs

YLabs is a leading international organization focused on designing technologies and solutions that amplify youth power, opportunity, agency, and wellbeing.

Founded in 2014 at the Harvard Innovation Lab, YLabs works closely with young people to co-create inclusive digital products and services that address some of the world’s most pressing social challenges.

The organization combines expertise in:

  • Youth-centered design
  • Adolescent health
  • Climate resilience
  • Livelihoods and economic opportunity
  • Behavioral science
  • Epidemiology
  • Digital product development
  • Public health innovation

YLabs has worked across 18 countries and maintains operations in:

  • Rwanda
  • United States

Their work focuses on three primary impact areas:

  1. Optimum health and wellness
  2. Improved economic opportunity
  3. Increased climate resilience

About AskingforaFriend.org (AFAF)

AskingforaFriend.org is a youth-centered digital platform designed specifically for adolescents aged 12–19 years.

The platform was co-designed with more than 300 young people in California to help adolescents learn about, access, and utilize sensitive wellness resources.

The platform supports youth in areas such as:

  • Sexual health
  • Mental health
  • Identity affirmation
  • Gender-based violence support
  • Emotional wellbeing
  • Youth-centered healthcare navigation

The initiative particularly prioritizes historically marginalized and underserved youth communities.

YLabs is now preparing to expand the platform nationally across the United States to support even more young people.

About the Youth Leadership Fellow Position

The Youth Leadership Fellow will play a major role in supporting the national expansion of AskingforaFriend.org.

The selected fellow will work alongside:

  • YLabs experts
  • Youth leaders
  • International fellows
  • Public health professionals
  • Digital campaign teams

This role is ideal for a young person passionate about youth advocacy, digital health, storytelling, wellness access, and community engagement.

Fellowship Overview

Category Details
Organization YLabs
Position Youth Leadership Fellow
Employment Type Fellow
Location United States (Remote)
Duration June – October 2026
Time Commitment 50% – 100%
Compensation USD 5,000 per month
Experience Level Entry-level
Application Deadline 05 June 2026

Fellowship Responsibilities

The selected fellow will contribute to a variety of youth-centered activities and national engagement initiatives.

1. Develop Youth Wellness Content

The fellow will help create:

  • Decision support tools
  • Health quizzes
  • FAQ responses
  • Informational wellness content

The content will respond directly to issues and concerns submitted by young people through the platform.

Topics may include:

  • Mental health
  • Sexual wellness
  • Healthy relationships
  • Identity support
  • Emotional wellbeing
  • Safety and support services

2. Support Partnership Development

The fellow will help identify and engage organizations and individuals aligned with the mission of AskingforaFriend.org.

This includes:

  • Building strategic relationships
  • Expanding outreach efforts
  • Strengthening youth support networks
  • Supporting collaborative initiatives

3. Contribute to National Marketing Campaigns

The selected fellow will support the creation of a national awareness campaign as the platform expands beyond California.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Creating paid and organic social media content
  • Youth-centered storytelling
  • Campaign messaging
  • Digital engagement
  • Marketing outreach strategies

4. Advocacy and Public Engagement

The fellow may also support:

  • Webinars
  • Conference presentations
  • Youth advocacy initiatives
  • Public awareness activities
  • Platform promotion

This role provides significant opportunities for leadership development and public speaking experience.

Who Should Apply?

YLabs encourages applications from young people who are deeply passionate about youth wellbeing and social impact.

The organization specifically welcomes applicants who:

  • Care about helping young people access support and information
  • Have experience advocating for youth issues
  • Have studied health, education, or public health topics
  • Have volunteered in youth-centered initiatives
  • Possess strong communication skills
  • Understand the needs of marginalized youth communities

Importantly, YLabs emphasizes that applicants do not need traditional credentials or highly polished resumes to qualify.

The organization values:

  • Lived experience
  • Authenticity
  • Passion
  • Creativity
  • Community connection
  • Leadership potential

Desired Qualifications

Applicants should ideally demonstrate:

Strong Communication Skills

  • Excellent writing abilities
  • Ability to communicate with adolescents
  • Clear storytelling skills
  • Public engagement abilities

Passion for Youth and Digital Health

Candidates should show interest in:

  • Youth advocacy
  • Digital health
  • Public health education
  • Social impact
  • Wellness innovation
  • Community support

Personal Connection to the Mission

YLabs strongly values applicants whose lived experiences align with the communities the platform serves.

Applicants are encouraged to reflect on:

  • Whether AskingforaFriend.org addresses needs they or their communities have experienced
  • Why this work matters personally to them

Compensation and Benefits

Monthly Compensation

The fellowship offers:

USD 5,000 per month (full-time equivalent)

Compensation is commensurate with experience.

Additional Fellowship Benefits

Participants will gain:

  • Leadership experience
  • National advocacy exposure
  • Public health communication skills
  • Digital campaign experience
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Collaboration experience with international experts
  • Experience in youth-centered design and innovation

The fellowship can significantly strengthen future careers in:

  • Public health
  • Youth development
  • Advocacy
  • Communications
  • Nonprofit leadership
  • Behavioral science
  • Social innovation
  • Community engagement

Required Application Materials

Applicants must submit the following:

1. Resume

A current resume outlining education, experience, and relevant involvement.

2. Short Video Submission (2–5 Minutes)

Applicants must upload a short video answering several prompts.

The video should include:

  • Introduction about yourself
  • Why you care about Asking for a Friend
  • Why you want to become the Youth Leadership Fellow
  • Why you are the best person for the role
  • Ideas for new quizzes, FAQs, or support tools the platform could include

Video Submission Instructions

Applicants should:

  1. Upload the video to:
    • Google Drive
    • YouTube
  2. Ensure accessibility settings allow viewing.
  3. Paste the accessible link into the application form.

YLabs notes that professional editing is not required. Applicants are encouraged to focus on authenticity and clarity.

Information Session

Interested applicants can attend an informational session to learn more about the role and how to get involved.

Session Details

Event Details
Date 29 May 2026
Time 11:00 AM PST
Format Online

Equal Opportunity Commitment

YLabs strongly promotes equity, diversity, and inclusion.

The organization encourages applications from people of all:

  • Races
  • Cultures
  • Educational backgrounds
  • Socio-economic groups
  • Gender identities
  • Sexual orientations
  • Physical abilities
  • Age groups

YLabs does not discriminate based on protected characteristics under applicable laws.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 05 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early.

How to Apply

Applicants can learn more and submit applications through the official YLabs careers platform

Apply Here 

For more information on this fellowship, click here 

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here 

The post YLabs Youth Leadership Fellow 2026: Remote Fellowship Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Digital Health and Youth Advocacy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Change Collective 2026 — Fully Funded Intergenerational Climate Leadership Programme in Nature (UK)
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Change Collective 2026 is a powerful intergenerational learning and reflection programme designed for people working in climate and environmental change, offering space to rethink purpose, direction, and collective action in a time of global uncertainty. This initiative is co-hosted with Jonathon Porritt, a leading voice in sustainability and environmental strategy, and brings together experienced […]

The post Change Collective 2026 — Fully Funded Intergenerational Climate Leadership Programme in Nature (UK) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Change Collective 2026 is a powerful intergenerational learning and reflection programme designed for people working in climate and environmental change, offering space to rethink purpose, direction, and collective action in a time of global uncertainty.

This initiative is co-hosted with Jonathon Porritt, a leading voice in sustainability and environmental strategy, and brings together experienced practitioners, activists, policymakers, and young changemakers.

At a time when young people are facing an economic crisis, accelerating climate impacts, political instability, and rising global uncertainty, this programme creates a rare opportunity to pause, reflect, and reimagine action with clarity and resilience.

Importantly, 2 fully funded bursary places are available for 18–25 year olds, making this an exceptional opportunity for emerging climate leaders.

About the Change Collective Programme

The Change Collective is a nature-based leadership and reflection programme designed for individuals already engaged in climate and environmental work across sectors including:

  • Activism and campaigning
  • Policy and governance
  • Business and corporate sustainability
  • Community organising
  • Environmental advocacy and education

It is built around one central question:

“What now, and what is mine to do?”

This question guides all learning, reflection, and peer exchange throughout the programme.

Core Purpose of the Programme

The Change Collective is designed to help participants:

  • Gain clarity in a complex and rapidly changing world
  • Strengthen emotional and professional resilience
  • Build meaningful peer-to-peer networks
  • Reconnect with purpose and direction
  • Develop collective intelligence for climate action
  • Step back from urgency and reconnect with strategic thinking

Programme Structure Overview

The programme combines a 3-day residential retreat in nature with a 3-month online community of practice.

1. Three-Day Residential Retreat (In-Person)

📍 Location:

Sheepdrove Organic Farm, Berkshire, United Kingdom

📅 Dates:

Wednesday 1 July – Friday 3 July 2026

What Happens During the Residential:

Participants engage in immersive reflection and facilitated dialogue in a natural environment designed to support clarity and transformation.

Key elements include:

  • Deep reflective sessions on personal and collective roles
  • Structured conversations on climate systems and change
  • Guided nature immersion experiences
  • Group dialogue and peer learning
  • Emotional and strategic reflection on climate work
  • Time away from institutional pressure and urgency

The residential is structured around three core lenses:

Me — Personal Reflection

  • What in me needs to evolve for this moment?
  • What habits or patterns no longer serve?
  • What needs to end for new clarity to emerge?

You — Relational Awareness

  • How do I relate to others under pressure?
  • Where is trust strong or weak?
  • How do I navigate fear, fatigue, and tension?

Us — Collective Systems

  • What systems do I operate within?
  • What are their limits and strengths?
  • How can collective agency be strengthened?

2. Three-Month Community of Practice (Online)

After the residential, participants continue in an extended learning journey through structured online engagement.

Key Components:

  • Three group online workshops:
    • 24 July 2026
    • 11 September 2026
    • 25 September 2026
  • Fortnightly peer coaching sessions (4 total)
  • Monthly action learning groups (3 sessions)
  • Structured reflection and accountability spaces
  • Peer collaboration and feedback loops

Focus of the Online Phase:

  • Translating reflection into real-world action
  • Strengthening peer support systems
  • Developing collaborative climate initiatives
  • Exploring strategic dilemmas together
  • Maintaining long-term accountability and clarity

What Participants Gain

By the end of the programme, participants will leave with:

  • Clearer understanding of their role in climate and environmental systems
  • Improved emotional resilience in the face of uncertainty
  • Stronger decision-making and strategic clarity
  • Tools for relational and collective leadership
  • Renewed energy and motivation for climate work
  • A trusted peer network of climate practitioners
  • Greater confidence in navigating complexity and change

Why This Programme Matters

The Change Collective responds directly to the reality that:

  • Climate impacts are accelerating globally
  • Political systems are increasingly unstable
  • Institutions are under pressure and often failing
  • Many climate professionals feel burnout and uncertainty
  • Collective action requires deeper collaboration and reflection

By combining nature immersion, reflective dialogue, and structured peer learning, the programme creates space for deeper thinking and more grounded action.

Venue: Sheepdrove Organic Farm

The residential takes place at Sheepdrove Organic Farm, a certified organic and environmentally restored landscape in the North Wessex Downs.

The site includes:

  • Chalk grasslands
  • Ancient woodlands
  • Wetlands and reedbeds
  • Biodiversity-rich ecosystems
  • Sustainable farming systems

Accommodation options include:

  • Single rooms
  • Twin rooms
  • Camping options

The natural environment plays a central role in the programme by supporting:

  • Reduced stress
  • Improved clarity and focus
  • Better emotional regulation
  • Deeper systems thinking

Facilitators and Leadership Team

The programme is convened by experienced leaders in climate strategy, facilitation, and systems change:

  • Jonathon Porritt — environmental campaigner and sustainability strategist
  • Chloe Revill — facilitator and climate diplomacy expert
  • Andy Raingold — leadership coach and transformation facilitator

Together, they bring experience across:

  • Climate policy and diplomacy
  • Civil society and activism
  • Government advisory work
  • Organisational transformation
  • Nature-based leadership development

Why Nature Is Central

Nature is not just a setting—it is a core methodology of the programme.

Being in nature helps participants:

  • Reduce cognitive overload
  • Improve attention and clarity
  • Step away from urgency-driven thinking
  • Reconnect with long-term systems thinking
  • Reflect on complexity with greater calm

It supports more grounded decision-making in high-pressure environments.

Key Dates

  • Residential: 1–3 July 2026
  • Online Workshop 1: 24 July 2026
  • Online Workshop 2: 11 September 2026
  • Online Workshop 3: 25 September 2026
  • Application deadline: Rolling / bursary-based (via links provided)

Click here for more and to apply.

For more global scholarship opportunities Click here

The post Change Collective 2026 — Fully Funded Intergenerational Climate Leadership Programme in Nature (UK) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EleutherAI Summer of Open AI Research 2026: Fully Online AI Research Mentorship Program for Aspiring Researchers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Aspiring AI researchers, programmers, students, and open science enthusiasts from around the world now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on research experience through the EleutherAI Summer of Open AI Research (SOAR) 2026 program. The Summer of Open AI Research is a five-week, fully online mentorship and research initiative sponsored by EleutherAI, one of the […]

The post EleutherAI Summer of Open AI Research 2026: Fully Online AI Research Mentorship Program for Aspiring Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Aspiring AI researchers, programmers, students, and open science enthusiasts from around the world now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on research experience through the EleutherAI Summer of Open AI Research (SOAR) 2026 program.

The Summer of Open AI Research is a five-week, fully online mentorship and research initiative sponsored by EleutherAI, one of the leading organisations in open-source artificial intelligence research.

The programme is designed to help individuals with little or no prior research experience contribute to meaningful AI research projects under the mentorship of experienced researchers and experts in the field.

Participants will collaborate on real-world open science projects in areas such as:

  • AI interpretability
  • AI safety
  • Representation learning
  • Mechanistic interpretability
  • Scientific reasoning
  • AI for science
  • Information retrieval
  • Autonomous systems
  • Audio and generative modelling

Successful participants will contribute directly to active research projects and may receive formal credit on resulting publications and research outputs.

Applications for the Summer of Open AI Research 2026 are open until 8 June 2026.

What Is the EleutherAI Summer of Open AI Research (SOAR)?

The Summer of Open AI Research (SOAR) is an online AI research programme focused on:

  • Open science
  • Collaborative AI development
  • Research mentorship
  • Skill development
  • Community-driven innovation

The initiative connects aspiring researchers with experienced mentors to work collaboratively on open-source AI research projects over five weeks.

The programme seeks to lower barriers to AI research participation by welcoming:

  • Students
  • Self-taught learners
  • Programmers
  • Independent researchers
  • Individuals outside traditional academic systems

SOAR creates opportunities for participants to gain:

  • Structured mentorship
  • Practical research experience
  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Publication experience
  • Exposure to cutting-edge AI topics

About EleutherAI

EleutherAI is a prominent open-source AI research organisation known for advancing collaborative and transparent artificial intelligence research.

The organisation focuses on:

  • Open-source AI models
  • Language modelling
  • AI interpretability
  • Alignment research
  • Mechanistic interpretability
  • Safety research
  • Community-driven AI innovation

Unlike proprietary AI ecosystems, EleutherAI promotes open and collaborative research accessible to the global AI community.

The “Open AI” in the programme title refers to open and collaborative artificial intelligence research, not the company behind ChatGPT.

Programme Overview

The Summer of Open AI Research is fully online and hosted through the EleutherAI Discord community platform.

Programme Duration

  • 5 weeks

Programme Format

  • Fully remote
  • Online collaboration
  • Mentorship-based learning
  • Research-focused teamwork

Participants will work closely with mentors and research groups on selected projects while developing practical AI research skills.

Who Should Apply?

EleutherAI encourages applications from individuals interested in contributing to open-source AI research.

Ideal applicants include:

  • Experienced programmers interested in AI research
  • BS, MS, and PhD students
  • Computer science students
  • Mathematics students
  • Physics students
  • Self-taught AI researchers
  • Independent learners seeking mentorship
  • Open science contributors

Importantly, applicants do not need prior research experience to apply.

The programme explicitly encourages individuals outside traditional academic environments to participate.

Eligibility Requirements

According to EleutherAI:

  • Anyone can apply
  • Applicants will be evaluated based on their ability to contribute
  • Research experience is not mandatory

Applicants are expected to demonstrate:

  • Technical curiosity
  • Programming ability
  • Commitment to collaborative work
  • Interest in AI research

Research Areas and Project Tracks

Participants can apply to projects across multiple AI research domains.

Interpretability and Reasoning Projects

Research topics include:

  • Detecting reasoning errors in open-weight models
  • Evaluating interpretability methods
  • Semantic compression in foundation models
  • Hierarchy analysis in sparse autoencoders
  • Activation verbalizer experiments
  • Tracing subliminal learning during pretraining

AI Safety Projects

Participants may work on:

  • Subliminal prompting mechanisms
  • Alignment and interpretability
  • Behavioural shift prediction
  • Deceptive compliance in AI agents

AI Applications Projects

Project areas include:

  • AI for astronomy
  • Information retrieval systems
  • Autonomous drone simulations
  • Singing voice synthesis models

These projects provide practical exposure to modern AI research methodologies and tools.

Example Research Project

One highlighted project is:

Detecting Right-Answer, Wrong-Reason Behavior in Open-Weight Reasoning Models

This project examines whether AI models can arrive at correct answers using flawed reasoning processes.

Participants will:

  • Create reasoning problem datasets
  • Analyse model behaviour
  • Compare reasoning outputs
  • Study sparse autoencoder features
  • Evaluate internal evidence for genuine reasoning

Skills Required for Participation

While research experience is not required, participants are expected to have some technical foundation.

Preferred skills include:

  • Python programming
  • Basic machine learning knowledge
  • Familiarity with PyTorch
  • Familiarity with Hugging Face tools
  • Data analysis experience
  • Git and GitHub workflows
  • Ability to read technical materials carefully

Strong experimental thinking and analytical curiosity are especially valuable.

Mentorship and Collaboration

Participants will work under the mentorship of experienced researchers from institutions and organisations such as:

  • MIT CSAIL
  • Stanford University
  • EleutherAI
  • Constellation Institute
  • Algoverse

The mentorship model is one of the programme’s strongest features, offering participants direct guidance while working on practical AI research problems.

Participant Benefits

The SOAR programme offers multiple academic and professional benefits.

Research Experience

Participants gain:

  • Hands-on AI research exposure
  • Real-world project experience
  • Collaborative research training

Mentorship

Selected participants receive:

  • Guidance from experienced researchers
  • Structured learning support
  • Exposure to advanced AI topics

Publication and Recognition

Participants may:

  • Receive project credit
  • Contribute to publishable research
  • Build academic and technical portfolios

Networking Opportunities

Participants can connect with:

  • AI researchers
  • Students
  • Open-source developers
  • Technical communities worldwide

Open Science Contribution

Participants contribute directly to:

  • Open-source AI development
  • Collaborative scientific research
  • Publicly accessible AI innovation

Programme Timeline

Important Dates

15 May 2026

Project proposal deadline

18 May 2026

Participant applications open

8 June 2026

Participant application deadline

5 July 2026

Application decisions released

Rolling decisions may arrive earlier.

6 July 2026

Project preparation begins

13 July 2026

Main programme begins

1 August 2026

Short research talks and progress presentations

16 August 2026

Final talks and programme conclusion

Time Commitment

The required time commitment depends on the selected project.

Each project listing provides estimated weekly hours.

For example:

  • Some projects may require approximately 6 hours per week

Participants should review project requirements carefully before applying.

Why This Programme Matters

AI research opportunities are often limited to individuals within elite academic institutions or established research labs.

The Summer of Open AI Research programme seeks to make AI research more accessible by:

  • Supporting independent learners
  • Encouraging open collaboration
  • Expanding research access globally
  • Promoting mentorship-based learning
  • Strengthening open science ecosystems

For many participants, SOAR may become:

  • Their first AI research experience
  • A pathway into advanced research careers
  • An opportunity to build professional networks
  • A stepping stone toward publication and graduate research

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply online before the deadline.

 Apply here

Final Thoughts

The EleutherAI Summer of Open AI Research 2026 programme presents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers, programmers, students, and independent learners to gain practical AI research experience within a collaborative and mentorship-driven environment.

With projects spanning interpretability, AI safety, representation learning, and advanced AI applications, participants will engage directly with cutting-edge research while contributing to the broader open science movement.

For individuals interested in entering the world of artificial intelligence research, the SOAR programme offers both valuable technical development and meaningful community engagement in one of the most dynamic fields in technology today.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post EleutherAI Summer of Open AI Research 2026: Fully Online AI Research Mentorship Program for Aspiring Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Geneva Peace Fellowship Programme 2026.
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The International Geneva Peace Fellowship Programme 2026 is a highly competitive, residential leadership initiative designed to develop the next generation of global experts in peacebuilding, humanitarian diplomacy, mediation, and inclusive international cooperation. This Fellowship is jointly implemented by the Interpeace and the Geneva Graduate Institute, with support from the Foundation for the Adaptation of International […]

The post International Geneva Peace Fellowship Programme 2026. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Geneva Peace Fellowship Programme 2026 is a highly competitive, residential leadership initiative designed to develop the next generation of global experts in peacebuilding, humanitarian diplomacy, mediation, and inclusive international cooperation.

This Fellowship is jointly implemented by the Interpeace and the Geneva Graduate Institute, with support from the Foundation for the Adaptation of International Geneva (FAGI).

Based in Geneva, Switzerland, the programme places selected Fellows directly inside one of the world’s most influential diplomatic ecosystems—International Geneva—where global policies, humanitarian responses, and peace processes are shaped.

Only 10 Fellows worldwide will be selected for the 2026 cohort, making this one of the most selective peace and diplomacy fellowships globally.

About the International Geneva Peace Fellowship Programme

The Fellowship is a 9-month full-time residential programme (September 2026 – June 2027) designed to combine:

  • Real institutional work in global organizations
  • Residential learning and collaboration
  • Applied peacebuilding research
  • Professional mentorship
  • Leadership development

It is not a theoretical academic programme. Instead, it is a hands-on professional immersion in global peace and humanitarian systems.

Core Purpose of the Programme

The Fellowship is built to respond to urgent global challenges such as:

  • Fragmentation of global conflicts
  • Weakening multilateral cooperation systems
  • Limited entry points into peace and diplomacy careers
  • Growing demand for innovative peace leadership

Key Objectives:

  • Train emerging global peace leaders
  • Strengthen humanitarian diplomacy capacity
  • Build cross-regional leadership networks
  • Support innovation in peacebuilding practices
  • Reinforce Geneva as a global peace hub
  • Bridge research, policy, and field operations

Programme Structure (Three Core Pillars)

1. Residential Programme in Geneva

Fellows live together in Domaine Barton (Lake Geneva) while participating in intensive professional development.

Key Activities:

  • Living in a fully immersive residential environment
  • Participation in peace and diplomacy projects
  • Study visits across International Geneva institutions
  • Collaborative research and policy work
  • Peer-to-peer leadership development

This environment is designed to build strong global collaboration and leadership capacity.

2. Institutional Collaboration & Placements

Each Fellow is placed in a leading international organization in Geneva for hands-on experience.

What Fellows Do:

  • Work on real-time peace and humanitarian projects
  • Support institutional programmes and policy work
  • Engage in diplomatic and research activities
  • Receive mentorship from senior professionals
  • Build long-term career networks

Key Partner Institutions:

  • Centre for Humanitarian Dialogue
  • Kofi Annan Foundation
  • Centre for Civilians in Conflict (CIVIC)
  • International Federation of the Red Cross (IFRC)
  • World Council of Churches

This ensures direct exposure to real-world humanitarian diplomacy and peace operations.

3. Co-Creation, Research & Leadership Development

This pillar focuses on innovation, research, and strategic leadership building.

Key Components:

  • Intensive 1-week training programme at the start
  • Advanced learning in peace and humanitarian diplomacy
  • Development of applied research projects
  • Participation in policy innovation work
  • Continuous mentorship and peer exchange
  • Independent strategic project development

Fellows are encouraged to develop innovative peacebuilding solutions that can influence global policy and practice.

Thematic Focus Areas (2026 Cohort)

Each Fellow is selected under one of six thematic tracks aligned with global peace challenges.

1. New Frontiers of Peacemaking

Focuses on modern conflict environments and innovation in mediation.

Includes:

  • Addressing neglected conflicts
  • Building inclusive peace processes
  • Climate-related conflict risks
  • Digital and technological conflict dynamics
  • Gender-inclusive mediation approaches

2. Health and Peace

Explores the intersection between health systems and peacebuilding.

Includes:

  • Health systems in conflict zones
  • Climate and environmental health risks
  • Integrated peace-health frameworks
  • Strengthening global health governance

3. Ecumenical Peacebuilding and Faith-Based Diplomacy

Focuses on religious and interfaith contributions to peace.

Includes:

  • Faith-based mediation efforts
  • Interreligious dialogue and reconciliation
  • Role of churches and faith leaders in diplomacy
  • Community-level peacebuilding initiatives

4. Youth Leadership, Participation & Peacebuilding

Focuses on empowering young people in peace processes.

Includes:

  • Youth leadership development
  • Civic engagement in conflict prevention
  • Peace education and advocacy
  • Youth participation in policy processes

5. Protection of Civilians & Modern Conflict Dynamics

Focuses on civilian safety in evolving conflicts.

Includes:

  • Civilian protection strategies
  • Urban warfare and explosive weapons
  • AI and drone warfare risks
  • Forced displacement and humanitarian access
  • Protection of vulnerable populations

6. Women, Peace & Security (WPS) and Inclusive Peacebuilding

Focuses on gender equality in peace processes.

Includes:

  • Women’s participation in peace negotiations
  • Gender-responsive peacebuilding policies
  • Local women-led peace initiatives
  • Structural barriers to inclusion

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must fall into one of the following categories:

1. Recent Graduates

  • Master’s degree holders
  • OR PhD candidates (advanced stage)
  • Graduated within the last 12 months

2. Junior Professionals

  • 1–4 years of experience

3. Mid-Career Professionals

  • 5–10 years of experience

Required Qualifications

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Strong leadership potential in peace or humanitarian work
  • Background in international relations, law, development, or social sciences
  • OR relevant field or policy experience
  • Excellent English or French communication skills
  • Ability to work in multicultural environments
  • Adaptability to international field and institutional settings
  • Full availability for a 9-month residency in Geneva

Important Note:

This is NOT a PhD or academic research fellowship. It is a practice-based leadership programme combining policy, fieldwork, and applied research.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

1. CV

  • Maximum 2 pages

2. Cover Letter (max 500 words / 1 page)

Must include:

  • Preferred thematic area
  • Relevant leadership experience
  • Motivation for joining the Fellowship
  • A strategic idea or initiative to be developed during the programme

Application Deadline

7 June 2026 — 11:59 pm CET

Click here for more and to apply

For more global fellowship opportunities, Click here

The post International Geneva Peace Fellowship Programme 2026. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NATO Internship Programme 2027 in Rome, Italy: Fully Funded International Internship Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates from NATO Member Countries
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization has officially opened applications for the NATO Internship Programme 2027 at the prestigious NATO Defense College. This highly competitive international internship programme offers students and recent graduates from NATO member countries the opportunity to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s most influential international security and defense alliances. The […]

The post NATO Internship Programme 2027 in Rome, Italy: Fully Funded International Internship Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates from NATO Member Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization has officially opened applications for the NATO Internship Programme 2027 at the prestigious NATO Defense College. This highly competitive international internship programme offers students and recent graduates from NATO member countries the opportunity to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s most influential international security and defense alliances.

The internship programme will take place in Rome, Italy, with intake periods scheduled for February 2027 and August 2027.

This opportunity is ideal for ambitious young professionals seeking international exposure in areas related to international relations, security studies, diplomacy, defense policy, political affairs, strategic studies, and multinational cooperation.

Applications close on 22 June 2026.

About NATO

North Atlantic Treaty Organization is one of the world’s most important political and military alliances, bringing together member countries committed to collective defense, international peace, security cooperation, and democratic values.

Founded in 1949, NATO works to:

  • Maintain international peace and security
  • Promote democratic values
  • Encourage political consultation among member states
  • Address emerging security threats
  • Strengthen global defense cooperation
  • Support crisis management and conflict prevention

NATO plays a critical role in international diplomacy, strategic defense planning, cybersecurity, military cooperation, and geopolitical stability.

The alliance also invests heavily in research, education, leadership development, and international policy dialogue through institutions such as the NATO Defense College.

About the NATO Defense College (NDC)

NATO Defense College is NATO’s premier academic institution dedicated to higher-level strategic education and research.

Located in Rome, Italy, the college provides advanced education and training to military officers, diplomats, policymakers, and civilian officials from NATO member states and partner countries.

The institution focuses on:

  • International security studies
  • Strategic leadership
  • Defense policy
  • Political-military affairs
  • Geopolitical analysis
  • Crisis management
  • International cooperation

Interns selected for the programme will gain exposure to a dynamic international working environment and contribute to ongoing institutional activities within the college.

NATO Internship Programme 2027 Overview

Internship Details

Category Details
Organization NATO
Institution NATO Defense College
Location Rome, Italy
Intake Periods February 2027 & August 2027
Programme Type Internship
Application Deadline 22 June 2026

Why the NATO Internship Programme is Important

The NATO Internship Programme provides young professionals with a rare opportunity to understand the inner workings of international organizations focused on diplomacy, defense, policy development, and security cooperation.

Interns will have the opportunity to:

  • Gain professional experience in an international institution
  • Learn from experts in defense and strategic affairs
  • Develop cross-cultural communication skills
  • Build international professional networks
  • Enhance knowledge of global security issues
  • Strengthen policy research and analytical skills
  • Understand multinational cooperation frameworks

The programme also serves as a valuable stepping stone for careers in:

  • International relations
  • Diplomacy
  • Public policy
  • Security studies
  • Defense analysis
  • International organizations
  • Government service
  • Research institutions
  • Political affairs

Eligibility Requirements for the NATO Internship Programme 2027

Applicants must meet several strict eligibility conditions before applying.

1. Age Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be at least 21 years old
  • Be under 30 years old on the application closing date

This requirement ensures the programme remains focused on students and early-career professionals.

2. Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • Citizens of a NATO member country

Only nationals from NATO member states are eligible to apply for this internship opportunity.

3. Educational Requirement

Applicants must satisfy one of the following educational conditions:

  • Have completed at least two years of university study and currently be enrolled in a third year

OR

  • Have obtained their highest university degree no more than one year prior to application

The programme is therefore suitable for both current university students and recent graduates.

4. Language Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Proficiency in at least one of NATO’s official languages:
    • English
    • French

Additionally:

  • Working knowledge of the second official language is considered an advantage.
  • Knowledge of other languages may also strengthen an application for certain positions.

Strong communication skills in multilingual environments are highly valued within NATO institutions.

What Interns Can Expect

Selected interns will work in a highly multicultural and intellectually stimulating environment.

Participants may gain exposure to:

  • Strategic policy discussions
  • Research activities
  • International cooperation projects
  • Administrative and organizational tasks
  • Security and defense studies
  • Academic conferences and seminars
  • Policy drafting and analytical work

Interns will also have opportunities to interact with experts, diplomats, military officials, researchers, and policymakers from across NATO member countries.

Benefits of the NATO Internship Programme

Although the vacancy notice focuses mainly on eligibility and recruitment information, participating in the NATO Internship Programme offers several significant professional advantages.

Key Benefits Include:

  • International work experience
  • Exposure to NATO operations and structures
  • Networking with global professionals
  • Career development opportunities
  • Experience working in a multinational environment
  • Enhanced understanding of international security and diplomacy
  • Strong addition to academic and professional CVs
  • Access to a globally respected institution

The internship experience can significantly strengthen applications for future opportunities in government, diplomacy, international development, research, and security-related careers.

Equal Opportunity and Diversity Commitment

North Atlantic Treaty Organization emphasizes equal opportunity and non-discrimination throughout its recruitment process.

According to the official notice, NATO does not discriminate based on:

  • Gender
  • Race
  • Ethnic origin
  • Religion
  • Nationality
  • Disability
  • Sexual orientation
  • Age

The organization encourages applications from qualified individuals across all eligible backgrounds.

Application Process

Qualified candidates must apply through NATO’s official e-recruitment system.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Carefully review all eligibility requirements
  2. Prepare supporting documents in advance
  3. Submit applications before the deadline
  4. Regularly monitor the NATO Defense College website for updates

Application Deadline

Deadline: 22 June 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early due to the highly competitive nature of the programme.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can learn more and submit applications through the official NATO platforms

Apply here 

Why Students and Graduates Should Apply

The NATO Internship Programme is an outstanding opportunity for young people seeking international exposure in policy, diplomacy, security, and strategic affairs.

For students and graduates interested in global governance, international cooperation, and multinational institutions, this internship offers:

  • Real-world policy exposure
  • International networking opportunities
  • Career-building experience
  • Academic enrichment
  • Practical understanding of international organizations

Interns will develop valuable professional and intercultural skills while contributing to one of the world’s leading international alliances.

For more information on this internship, click here 

Discover more internship opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post NATO Internship Programme 2027 in Rome, Italy: Fully Funded International Internship Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates from NATO Member Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Women Call for Proposals 2026: Up to USD 200,000 Grant for Civil Society Organizations Fighting Online Violence Against Women and Girls in the Western Balkans
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
UN Women has officially launched a major regional Call for Proposals for Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) under the EU-funded programme focused on gender equality and ending violence against women in the Western Balkans. This important funding opportunity seeks qualified civil society organizations to design and implement a large-scale behavioural change and awareness-raising campaign addressing online […]

The post UN Women Call for Proposals 2026: Up to USD 200,000 Grant for Civil Society Organizations Fighting Online Violence Against Women and Girls in the Western Balkans appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
UN Women has officially launched a major regional Call for Proposals for Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) under the EU-funded programme focused on gender equality and ending violence against women in the Western Balkans.

This important funding opportunity seeks qualified civil society organizations to design and implement a large-scale behavioural change and awareness-raising campaign addressing online and technology-facilitated violence against women and girls across the Western Balkans region.

The initiative falls under the regional programme:

“EU4 Gender Equality – Women’s Economic Empowerment and Ending Violence Against Women in the Western Balkans”

The selected organization will receive funding ranging between USD 180,000 and USD 200,000 to carry out strategic communication, advocacy, awareness, and behavioural change initiatives aimed at combating violence against women and girls, particularly in digital spaces.

Applications must be submitted by 12 June 2026 at 12:00 CET.

About UN Women

UN Women is the United Nations entity dedicated to advancing gender equality and empowering women worldwide.

The organization works globally to:

  • End violence against women and girls
  • Promote women’s economic empowerment
  • Advance women’s leadership and participation
  • Strengthen gender-responsive governance
  • Support feminist movements and civil society
  • Promote equal rights and opportunities

UN Women collaborates with governments, civil society organizations, development partners, regional institutions, and grassroots groups to address structural inequalities and create inclusive societies.

About the EU4 Gender Equality Regional Programme

The programme “EU4 Gender Equality – Women’s Economic Empowerment and Ending Violence Against Women in the Western Balkans” is funded by the European Union and implemented in partnership with UN Women.

The programme focuses on addressing critical gender equality challenges in the Western Balkans region, including:

  • Violence against women and girls
  • Online harassment and abuse
  • Economic inequalities
  • Social norms and harmful stereotypes
  • Gender discrimination
  • Limited access to services and support systems

The programme seeks to strengthen regional cooperation, public awareness, and institutional responses to gender-based violence.

Purpose of the Call for Proposals

UN Women is seeking a qualified Civil Society Organization (CSO) to serve as a Responsible Party responsible for implementing a regional behavioural change communication initiative.

The selected organization will develop and execute a comprehensive awareness campaign targeting violence against women and girls, with a specific focus on:

  • Online violence
  • Technology-facilitated abuse
  • Digital harassment
  • Cyber violence
  • Gender-based violence in digital spaces

The initiative aims to improve awareness, shift harmful attitudes, increase knowledge of rights, and strengthen access to available support services across the Western Balkans.

Key Objectives of the Programme

The selected CSO will help achieve several major objectives.

1. Raise Awareness on Violence Against Women and Girls

The programme seeks to increase public understanding of the various forms of violence women and girls experience both offline and online.

This includes awareness around:

  • Cyber harassment
  • Digital stalking
  • Online hate speech
  • Non-consensual image sharing
  • Gender-based online abuse
  • Technology-enabled intimidation

2. Promote Behavioural Change

A major focus of the programme is changing harmful attitudes, stereotypes, and behaviours that contribute to violence against women and girls.

The campaign is expected to encourage:

  • Respectful online behaviour
  • Gender equality
  • Positive social norms
  • Digital safety
  • Accountability
  • Community engagement

3. Increase Knowledge of Rights and Support Services

The campaign should also help women and girls better understand:

  • Their legal rights
  • Available support systems
  • Protection mechanisms
  • Reporting channels
  • Access to services and assistance

This is particularly important for vulnerable populations affected by online violence and discrimination.

4. Support Regional Advocacy Efforts

In addition to campaign implementation, the selected CSO will contribute to the creative communication concepts for the:

Regional Forum on Ending Violence Against Women and Girls

Specifically, the organization will support communication strategies for the:

  • 2026 edition
  • 2027 edition

This provides an opportunity for the selected organization to contribute to high-level regional advocacy and policy conversations.

Funding Amount

The budget range for the proposal is:

USD 180,000 – USD 200,000

Important notes:

  • Proposals exceeding the maximum amount will not be considered.
  • Applicants should ensure budgets align with programme expectations and deliverables.

This significant funding opportunity can support large-scale regional communication and advocacy activities.

Who Should Apply?

UN Women is specifically inviting:

  • Civil Society Organizations (CSOs)

Organizations with experience in gender equality, women’s rights, digital safety, communications, behavioural change campaigns, advocacy, and regional programme implementation are particularly well positioned for this opportunity.

Expected Areas of Expertise

The selected organization is expected to demonstrate strong expertise in areas such as:

  • Gender equality programming
  • Ending violence against women
  • Behavioural change communication
  • Public awareness campaigns
  • Advocacy and outreach
  • Digital communication
  • Regional programme implementation
  • Human rights
  • Online safety and cyber violence
  • Community engagement

Experience working in the Western Balkans region may provide an added advantage.

Geographic Focus

The programme specifically targets the:

Western Balkans

Campaign activities and outreach efforts are expected to address populations across the region.

Information Session for Applicants

UN Women will organize an online information session to help interested organizations understand the proposal requirements and application process.

Information Session Details

Event Details
Format Zoom
Date 1 June 2026
Time 12:00 CET

Applicants can register in advance through the official registration platform provided by UN Women.

After registration, participants will receive confirmation details and instructions for joining the session.

Important Administrative Requirements

Selected organizations must:

  • Accept all legal terms outlined in the Partner Agreement
  • Register and undergo verification on the UN Partner Portal (UNPP)

The UNPP is an online collaboration platform used by UN agencies to manage partnerships with civil society organizations.

Organizations should prepare early for registration and verification processes if not already registered.

Proposal Submission Deadline

Deadline: 12 June 2026 at 12:00 CET

UN Women emphasizes that proposals must be received before the official deadline.

Late submissions may not be considered.

Contact Information

For additional inquiries, interested organizations may contact:

Contact Person:

Natalija Ostojic

Email:

ecaro.cfp@unwomen.org

Why This Opportunity Matters

Technology-facilitated violence against women and girls is becoming one of the fastest-growing forms of gender-based violence globally.

This UN Women initiative recognizes the urgent need for:

  • Public education
  • Digital safety awareness
  • Behavioural transformation
  • Survivor-centered support
  • Regional cooperation
  • Advocacy and prevention strategies

The programme presents a major opportunity for civil society organizations to shape regional conversations and strengthen protections for women and girls in digital spaces.

How to Apply

Interested organizations should carefully review all proposal documents and annexes provided by UN Women before preparing submissions.

Apply here 

Applicants can learn more about this grant on here 

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, click here 

 

The post UN Women Call for Proposals 2026: Up to USD 200,000 Grant for Civil Society Organizations Fighting Online Violence Against Women and Girls in the Western Balkans appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNCCD COP17 Youth Delegation 2026 in Mongolia: Open Call for Young Leaders and Climate Changemakers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Young leaders, environmental advocates, youth organisations, and aspiring changemakers from around the world now have an exciting opportunity to participate in one of the most important global environmental conferences focused on land restoration, drought resilience, and sustainable development. The United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD) has officially launched the Open Delegation Call for Youth […]

The post UNCCD COP17 Youth Delegation 2026 in Mongolia: Open Call for Young Leaders and Climate Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders, environmental advocates, youth organisations, and aspiring changemakers from around the world now have an exciting opportunity to participate in one of the most important global environmental conferences focused on land restoration, drought resilience, and sustainable development.

The United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD) has officially launched the Open Delegation Call for Youth Participation at COP17 and the UNCCD Youth Forum 2026, which will take place in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, from 17 to 28 August 2026.

Held under the theme “Restoring Land, Restoring Hope,” UNCCD COP17 is expected to bring together governments, civil society, youth leaders, environmental experts, and global stakeholders to address the growing challenges of:

  • Land degradation
  • Desertification
  • Climate resilience
  • Food insecurity
  • Sustainable livelihoods
  • Environmental sustainability

The conference comes at a time when more than three billion people worldwide are affected by land degradation and declining ecosystems, making this summit a critical platform for global action and youth participation.

The UNCCD Youth Forum 2026, which will take place on 18 and 19 August 2026, aims to strengthen the meaningful engagement of young people in environmental policy discussions and implementation efforts while promoting youth-led eco-entrepreneurship and agribusiness innovation.

This open delegation call offers eligible young people and youth organisations the opportunity to participate in COP17 activities and potentially receive partial financial support should funding become available.

About UNCCD COP17 2026

The seventeenth session of the Conference of the Parties (COP17) to the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification is one of the world’s leading international gatherings focused on combating land degradation and strengthening drought resilience.

Event Details

  • Event: UNCCD COP17
  • Location: Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia
  • Dates: 17–28 August 2026
  • Theme: “Restoring Land, Restoring Hope”

The summit will serve as a major international platform where governments, policymakers, scientists, civil society organisations, and youth representatives collaborate on solutions for sustainable land management and environmental restoration.

The conference seeks to accelerate global action toward:

  • Land restoration targets
  • Drought resilience
  • Climate adaptation
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Ecosystem protection
  • Food security solutions

UNCCD Youth Forum 2026

Building on the success of previous Youth Forums organised during COP13, COP14, COP15, and COP16, the fifth UNCCD Youth Forum will take place alongside COP17.

Youth Forum Dates

18–19 August 2026

The Youth Forum aims to:

  • Empower young people in global environmental governance
  • Increase youth participation in UNCCD implementation
  • Strengthen youth advocacy
  • Promote youth-led environmental innovation
  • Support eco- and agripreneurship initiatives

The forum will also provide space for young leaders to:

  • Share experiences
  • Build international partnerships
  • Discuss environmental challenges
  • Advocate for supportive policies
  • Collaborate on sustainable development initiatives

Who Can Apply?

The open call is open to two major categories of applicants:

1. UNCCD Youth Accredited Organisations

Youth organisations accredited under the UNCCD framework may nominate representatives to participate in COP17 and the Youth Forum.

2. Individual Young Applicants

Young people may also nominate themselves independently for participation in:

  • UNCCD COP17
  • UNCCD Youth Forum 2026

This provides opportunities for youth leaders, climate advocates, environmental innovators, students, researchers, entrepreneurs, and grassroots organisers to engage directly in international environmental discussions.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants and nominees must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • Between 18 and 35 years old

Eligible Regions

Applicants must come from one of the following regions:

  • Africa
  • Asia-Pacific
  • Latin America and the Caribbean
  • Central and Eastern Europe
  • Western Europe and Others

Registration Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Already be registered for UNCCD COP17
    OR
  • Be willing to complete registration upon confirmation of participation

Who Is This Open Call Intended For?

The call specifically targets:

Young People Already Planning to Attend COP17

Individuals who have already secured participation arrangements for COP17 in Mongolia are encouraged to apply.

Young People Seeking Financial Support

Applicants who require partial financial assistance to attend the Youth Forum and COP17 are also encouraged to submit applications.

However, organisers have clarified that:

  • Funding has not yet been confirmed
  • Financial support is not guaranteed
  • Applicants are strongly encouraged to seek independent funding where possible

The information collected through the application process will help identify potential delegates should sponsorship opportunities become available.

Possible Financial Support

If funding becomes available, selected participants may receive partial participation support.

Potential Support Includes:

  • Shared accommodation
  • Travel assistance
  • Daily Subsistence Allowance (DSA)
  • Participation support during COP17
  • Support for attending the UNCCD Youth Forum

The support would cover activities taking place between:

  • 17–28 August 2026

including:

  • Youth Forum sessions
  • COP17 events
  • Networking opportunities
  • Side events and policy discussions

Important Funding Notice

Applicants should note carefully that:

  • Submission of the form does not guarantee sponsorship
  • Financial assistance depends entirely on funding availability
  • Delegates are encouraged to independently secure financial support whenever possible

This means applicants should proactively explore:

  • Institutional sponsorships
  • Grants
  • Youth participation funds
  • NGO support
  • Government travel support
  • University funding opportunities

Why This Opportunity Matters

Participation in UNCCD COP17 and the Youth Forum can provide young people with:

  • Global exposure
  • International networking opportunities
  • Policy engagement experience
  • Leadership development
  • Environmental advocacy skills
  • Collaboration opportunities with global stakeholders

Participants will gain firsthand experience engaging in:

  • International climate discussions
  • Sustainable development policymaking
  • Environmental governance
  • Youth-led advocacy
  • Global sustainability initiatives

This experience can significantly strengthen careers and initiatives related to:

  • Climate action
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Agriculture
  • Youth development
  • International policy
  • Sustainable innovation

Why Youth Participation Is Important

Young people are increasingly at the forefront of:

  • Climate advocacy
  • Land restoration campaigns
  • Sustainable agriculture innovation
  • Environmental entrepreneurship
  • Community resilience initiatives

The UNCCD Youth Forum recognises that youth voices are essential for building:

  • Sustainable futures
  • Inclusive environmental policies
  • Resilient communities
  • Innovative climate solutions

The forum therefore creates opportunities for young people to directly influence conversations around:

  • Environmental justice
  • Land restoration
  • Climate resilience
  • Sustainable livelihoods

Application Deadline

Deadline: 14 June 2026

Time: 23:59 CEST

Applicants are encouraged to apply before the deadline to ensure consideration.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application form.

Learn more and apply here: UNCCD COP17 Youth Delegation Application Form

Event Location

Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia
